subject: Ecology
title: Termites as Soil Builders
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Termites as Soil Builders.

hook.text: Termites are not just pests. In tropical soils, they are master architects and engineers.

buildup.text: Termite mounds mix deep minerals with organic matter, creating fertile soil patches.

discovery.text: Their tunnels extend meters underground, channeling water and aerating compacted layers.

twist.text: In African savannas, termite mounds act as nutrient islands that support unique plant communities.

climax.text: Remove termites from a tropical soil and decomposition slows dramatically.

punchline.text: Termites destroy houses but build ecosystems.

quiz.question: How do termite mounds affect tropical soils?
options: They mix minerals and organics, creating fertile patches
They only remove water from the soil
